entries.title: Trump tells BBC that King's visit could 'absolutely' help repair relations with UK
entries.snippet: In a phone interview with the BBC's North America editor, the president discussed next week's visit and his relationship with the UK PM.

entries.title: US boards ship carrying Iran oil as Trump threatens mine-laying vessels
entries.snippet: Central Command said it has intercepted 33 vessels since imposing a blockade on ships coming to and from Iran.

entries.title: Masked Iranian forces appear to seize ships in staged video
entries.snippet: Analysis indicates that parts of the video appear to have been filmed hours after the ships were reportedly seized.

entries.title: US soldier charged after winning $400,000 betting on removal of Maduro
entries.snippet: Gannon Ken Van Dyke allegedly made trades on Polymarket on the basis of classified information, the justice department says.

entries.title: Huge chunk of glacier blocks Everest route in peak climbing season
entries.snippet: Sherpas cannot prepare the route to the world's highest peak because a huge block of ice is in the way.

entries.title: Meta says it will cut 8,000 jobs as AI spending soars
entries.snippet: The cuts, which employees had been expecting for weeks, will be Meta's largest layoff since 2023.

entries.title: Singer D4vd had 'significant amount' of child sex abuse images when arrested, prosecutors say
entries.snippet: The Tiktok star's attorneys have maintained his innocence and vowed to "vigorously defend" him.

entries.title: Woman killed by bear in Polish forest, son and local government say
entries.snippet: In an extremely rare fatal encounter, the 58-year-old was killed as she was walking in a mountainous area, her son told rescuers.

entries.title: US government watchdog to investigate Epstein files release
entries.snippet: The justice department's inspector general will review the withholding and overdue release of secret files.

entries.title: More than 500 people killed in Tanzania election violence, inquiry finds
entries.snippet: Opposition parties accused security forces but the official report does not say who was responsible.

entries.title: US-Kuwaiti journalist held in Kuwait over social media posts acquitted, lawyers say
entries.snippet: Ahmed Shihab-Eldin reportedly faced charges, including spreading false information, after he re-posted images related to the Iran war.

entries.title: US Navy chief leaving post 'effective immediately', Pentagon says
entries.snippet: Reports say Phelan, whose role was largely administrative, left amid tension over US shipbuilding.

entries.title: South African police chief suspended over $20m health contract
entries.snippet: Cyril Ramaphosa said the suspension was because of the critical role Fannie Masemola plays in fighting crime.

entries.title: Two trains collide head-on in Denmark, leaving five critically hurt
entries.snippet: The accident happened early on Thursday at a level crossing north of Copenhagen.

entries.title: Warner Bros shareholders approve Paramount's $111bn takeover
entries.snippet: The approval came as Donald Trump is to attend a dinner with billionaire Paramount backers the Ellisons.

entries.title: MrBeast's company sued by ex-employee over sexual harassment claim
entries.snippet: Beast Industries has strongly denied claims of sexual harassment and workplace gender bias.

entries.title: Girl, 14, found in singer D4vd's car died of 'multiple injuries'
entries.snippet: The medical examiner said the 14-year-old's cause of death was determined months ago but was blocked from release.

entries.title: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
entries.snippet: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after IDF attacks on Red Cross vehicles also stopped rescuers from reaching the site.

entries.title: Trump administration reclassifies cannabis as less dangerous
entries.snippet: Medical marijuana was moved into a lower category of drugs in order to increase research and access.

entries.title: South African court orders Zambia to return former president's body
entries.snippet: Zambia authorities and Edgar Lungu's family are in a dispute over where the former leader's remains should be buried.

entries.title: U.S. soldier charged with suspected Polymarket insider trading over Maduro raid
entries.snippet: It's the first time suspected insider trading on Polymarket has led to criminal charges in the U.S.

entries.title: French police probe suspected weather device tampering after odd Polymarket bet
entries.snippet: The incident is the latest eyebrow-raising bet on Polymarket, as allegations of rigging and manipulation continue to haunt the popular prediction market site.

entries.title: The FDA gives the green light to the first gene therapy for deafness
entries.snippet: The treatment, developed by Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, is for a very rare form of deafness. But it represents a medical milestone.

entries.title: Meta will lay off 10% of its staff
entries.snippet: The cuts follow losses in two pivotal court cases and the company's push to invest in artificial intelligence.

entries.title: Airlines in Europe slash thousands of flights as Iran war cuts jet fuel supplies
entries.snippet: The soaring cost of jet fuel is forcing European airlines to cancel tens of thousands of flights, while energy authorities warned of a possible jet fuel shortage if supplies aren't replenished soon.

entries.title: Author details the spy network that took on America's post-WWII Nazi groups
entries.snippet: In The Secret War Against Hate , Steven J. Ross details the racist, anti-Semitic groups that sprang up in the latter half of the 20th century — and the spy network that worked to bring them to justice.

entries.title: Can a mentalist trick Trump? Oz Pearlman will try in a room full of journalists
entries.snippet: The White House Correspondents' Dinner will be headlined by a mentalist instead of a comedian. Oz Pearlman tells NPR he hopes to unify, delight and puzzle the crowd — but can't reveal how.

entries.title: How mosquitoes — and malaria — helped shape the whereabouts of early humankind
entries.snippet: A new study looks at an unexpected force that played a critical role in shaping the lives of ancient humans.

entries.title: Trump administration eases rules on some marijuana categories. Here's what to know
entries.snippet: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said he is immediately moving medical marijuana from Schedule I to Schedule III, which includes drugs like ketamine, Tylenol with codeine and anabolic steroids.

entries.title: Tensions rise in two ceasefires in the Middle East. And, the Navy secretary ousted
entries.snippet: Tensions are rising in the Middle East as shaky ceasefire agreements between the U.S., Israel and Iran, and Lebanon and Israel, are tested. And, the Secretary of the Navy is out of the role.

entries.title: Judge sets date for evidence against singer D4vd in killing of 14-year-old to be presented
entries.snippet: Lawyers for singer D4vd insisted Thursday on forcing prosecutors to show what evidence they have, and a judge set a hearing for next week for them to lay it out publicly in the killing and dismemberment of Celeste Rivas Hernandez.

entries.title: U.S. soldier charged with using classified intel to win more than $400K in bet on Maduro raid
entries.snippet: Gannon Ken Van Dyke was part of the operation to capture Maduro in January and used his access to classified information to make money on the prediction market site Polymarket, the federal prosecutor's office in New York said.

entries.title: WATCH: Trump announces Israel and Lebanon have agreed to 3-week ceasefire extension
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump said Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah by three weeks after talks at the White House on Thursday.

entries.title: Kalshi fines and suspends 3 congressional candidates for betting on their own elections
entries.snippet: Three congressional candidates wagered on the outcome of their own elections on Kalshi, according to the prediction market, which said Wednesday that it fined and suspended the men from their platform for five years.

entries.title: Trump in no rush to set deadline on Iran ceasefire extension
entries.snippet: Two days after President Trump extended his two-week ceasefire with Iran, the prospect of talks is giving way to more tensions and brinksmanship from both sides. Iran released video of forces taking control of two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, and President Trump wrote that he

entries.title: WATCH: Trump unveils deal with Regeneron to lower its drug prices for Medicaid
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump on Thursday announced a deal with drugmaker Regeneron to lower the cost of its pharmaceutical products as part of the White House's signature drug pricing initiative.

entries.title: Israel's UN ambassador says IDF is 'most moral military in the world'
entries.snippet: Officials from Israel and Lebanon met in Washington to build on last week's agreement for a 10-day ceasefire. Both sides pledged to halt offensive operations, while Lebanon committed to taking meaningful steps to prevent Hezbollah from launching attacks on Israel. Geoff Bennett s

entries.title: News Wrap: Wildfires threaten homes in southern Georgia
entries.snippet: In our news wrap Thursday, hundreds in Georgia have fled their homes as wildfires threaten areas in the southern part of the state, Warner Bros. shareholders voted to approve the company's $81 billion sale to Paramount and the European Union formally approved a loan package for U

entries.title: What marijuana's reclassification means for public health and businesses
entries.snippet: The federal government is reclassifying medical marijuana, categorizing it as a drug with potential medical benefits and less potential for harm. While this doesn't legalize marijuana nationally, it does open the door for further research into its effects. William Brangham report

entries.title: 'America First' aid policy reshapes how U.S. delivers global health assistance
entries.snippet: Since the dismantling of USAID, the Trump administration has been revamping aid policies, focusing on smaller deals with recipient governments. Countries receiving American aid will be required to finance part, and eventually, the entire program. Fred de Sam Lazaro reports from K

entries.title: State Department proposes sending Afghans who helped U.S. war effort to Congo
entries.snippet: Hundreds of Afghans who helped the United States' war effort in Afghanistan, and who are detained in Qatar, may soon be sent back to Afghanistan or the Democratic Republic of the Congo by the State Department. The push comes after President Trump halted the Afghan resettlement pr

entries.title: WATCH: Trump says he won't use nuclear weapons in Iran
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump said Thursday that he wouldn't use a nuclear weapon in Iran and that nobody should be allowed to use them.

entries.title: 'This is my home,' says DACA recipient deported then returned to U.S.
entries.snippet: The Department of Homeland Security says it has deported more than 675,000 immigrants in the first year of Trump's second term. Although the administration claims to be targeting violent criminals, others continue to be caught in the crackdown, including some who are protected fr

entries.title: Art exhibition shines light on Romani persecution during Holocaust
entries.snippet: It's a lesser-known chapter of the Holocaust, the murder of some 500,000 Roma and Sinti people, members of a long-marginalized and often persecuted minority in Europe. One way into that history is through the work of an artist who survived it herself. Jeffrey Brown reports for ou

entries.title: A brief history of the underground White House bunker at the heart of Trump's ballroom legal case
entries.snippet: Secrecy surrounding White House security makes details hard to come by, but President Donald Trump's court fight over his $400 million ballroom casts some light on an underground bunker at the site that has had a role in history.

entries.title: NTSB says firefighter heard 'stop, stop' before LaGuardia collision but didn't know who it was for
entries.snippet: A firefighter whose truck collided with an Air Canada jet last month on a runway at LaGuardia Airport in New York, killing both pilots, heard an air traffic controller warn "stop, stop, stop" but didn't know who it was for, federal investigators said Thursday.

entries.title: Gunfire inside Mall of Louisiana kills 1 person and wounds 5, Baton Rouge police say
entries.snippet: Two groups exchanged gunfire inside a food court Thursday at the Mall of Louisiana, killing one person and wounding five more, police said.

entries.title: California's woes at the center of debate among leading candidates for governor
entries.snippet: It's the first time in a generation that California has a wide-open contest for the heavily Democratic state's highest office, with more than 50 names on the ballot.

entries.title: Pope urges U.S. and Iran to resume peace talks, condemns capital punishment
entries.snippet: Pope Leo XIV urged the United States and Iran to return to talks to end the war Thursday and condemned capital punishment, in a wide-ranging press conference en route home from his trip to Africa.

entries.title: Federal authorities arrest 2 dozen Mexican Mafia members and associates in California
entries.snippet: More than two dozen members and associates of the Mexican Mafia were arrested Thursday during an early morning crackdown across Southern California, federal authorities said.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Trump says Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended by three weeks
entries.snippet: Donald Trump's remarks follow a meeting at the White House with high-ranking officials from Israel and Lebanon. The extension comes after Israel struck southern Lebanon and Hezbollah fired rockets at Israel. Follow DW.

entries.title: Warner Bros shareholders greenlight sale to Paramount
entries.snippet: The deal would combine two major Hollywood studios and bring CBS and CNN under one roof, tightening an already concentrated media landscape, critics say.

entries.title: What is Europe's plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz?
entries.snippet: France and the UK are leading efforts to establish a multinational mission to safeguard shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Questions remain about the scope of the operation and whether it can bring lasting stability.

entries.title: Ceasefire extended: What's next in the Iran war?
entries.snippet: With no fixed ceasefire deadline for now, the US and Iran are looking for ways to safeguard their own interests. DW takes a look at the main sticking points and explains some key issues.

entries.title: Is Ethiopia bracing for war?
entries.snippet: Analysts say the war in Iran has temporarily delayed an escalation in smoldering tensions between Ethiopia and Eritrea. But renewed armed conflict cannot be ruled out.

entries.title: Samsung workers rally, call for larger share of AI profits
entries.snippet: Unions at Samsung are threatening a weekslong strike if demands are not met. A production halt at one of the world's largest memory chipmakers could disrupt global chip supplies.

entries.title: UK: Health data volunteered by 500,000 people to Biobank charity listed for sale on Alibaba in China
entries.snippet: The British government is investigating how health data volunteered to a charity for research purposes wound up for sale via three vendors on the Alibaba e-commerce site in China. One listing had 500,000 people's data.

entries.title: Germany unveils first ever military strategy for Bundeswehr
entries.snippet: For the first time in its history, the Bundeswehr has adopted a military strategy to prepare Germany for future threats. Large sections of the document are classified.

entries.title: Libya oscillates between cooperation and illusion
entries.snippet: For the first time, representatives from both sides of Libya's political divide are participating in Flintlock, a multinational military exercise under US leadership. Is this a tentative sign of hope for reunification?

entries.title: Iran: Daily life shadowed by war, scarcity and fear
entries.snippet: The US naval blockade aims to put pressure on the Iranian regime, but ordinary Iranians mostly feel the effects. Many of them find their lives shaped by growing exhaustion.

entries.title: Pope Leo stressed importance of dialogue on Africa trip
entries.snippet: During a visit to four African countries, Pope Leo XIV called for peace, unity and dialogue. He criticized war, corruption and inequality, urging leaders to put people first.

entries.title: EU leaders discuss Iran, Ukraine at Cyprus summit
entries.snippet: The two days of talks started with the approval of a massive loan for Ukraine and are also expected to address the EU budget and the fallout from the conflict in the Middle East.

entries.title: How Ukraine is rebuilding its energy system under fire
entries.snippet: Since Chernobyl, Ukraine has lived in the shadow of nuclear disaster. Now, with Russia targeting its power grid, it's facing a new nuclear risk. The response is reshaping how the world thinks about energy in wartime.

entries.title: Unpacking the 2026 Venice Biennale controversies and highlights
entries.snippet: Following the return of Russia to the Biennale, the EU said it will carry out its threat to cut its funding from the international art show.

entries.title: Reza Pahlavi calls for regime change in Iran on Berlin visit
entries.snippet: The former Shah's son is in Germany to drum up support for his global campaign to overthrow Iran's leadership. During a speech in the capital, he called the German government's refusal to meet with him "a disgrace."

entries.title: Amnesty International paints a grim picture for human rights
entries.snippet: In its 2025/2026 report, the human rights watchdog Amnesty International calls out the "predatory behavior of the powerful" and calls for action to defend the global order.

entries.title: Germany news: Bavarian police arrest suspected spies
entries.snippet: Two men have been arrested in Bavaria and charged with espionage, Frankfurt Airport's new Terminal 3 has opened its doors, and former US President Obama has praised a German climate activist. DW has more.

entries.title: A booklover's dream holiday in Scotland
entries.snippet: As UNESCO celebrates World Book Day on April 23, we visit a Scottish coastal town that was saved by books.

entries.title: Bayern Munich on the brink of historic 'double treble'
entries.snippet: With Bundesliga titles in the bag and cup finals to come, Bayern's men and women have a chance to achieve something unique in Europe. The two teams dominate in Germany and appear united in their Champions League aims.
